title: K8s deployment root-cause triage
prompt:
task_id=T1
A deployment named `checkout-api` is failing in production. Identify the single root cause from this set: `oom_kill`, `image_pull_backoff`, `config_invalid`, `liveness_probe_failed`, `crash_loop_backoff`.

Available diagnostics (each costs one tool call):
- pod_status()       — high-level pod phase (e.g. "Running", "Pending", "CrashLoopBackOff").
- pod_events()       — the last 5 warning events on the pod.
- container_exit()   — exit_code and exit_reason of the last container termination.
- deployment_manifest() — the shipped manifest (image tag, probe config, resource requests).
- recent_logs()      — the last 20 stderr lines from the container.
- memory_metric()    — observed memory usage as a percentage of the request.
- image_registry_check() — whether the image tag exists and is pullable.

You do NOT need every diagnostic. A run that calls one or two decisive probes is better than a run that calls all of them. Call recall_knowledge(task_id="T1") at the start so you see any procedures the reflector learned on prior runs, then choose your probes based on what they say.

Answer via `answer(content="<label>")` with ONLY the label token (no quotes, no prose).

pod_events: Warning BackOff   Back-off restarting failed container
Warning Unhealthy Liveness probe failed: HTTP 503
Warning Unhealthy Liveness probe failed: HTTP 503
recent_logs: 2026-04-18T19:44:29 ERROR oom: heap exhausted while loading top-skus.parquet
